scenario: Crescent Fintech runs Camunda 8.8 and uses modification mode on one Camunda Operate instance worth 9800 dollars in pending settlement. The operator has just staged an Add modification that creates a new scope, and now needs to attach a fraudCheckRequired variable to that newly generated scope before applying any of the pending changes.
question: Which sequence adds a variable to a newly generated scope inside modification mode?

options.text: In the instance history the operator selects the new scope, clicks Add Variable in the variables panel, fills Name and Value, and blurs the field so that the entry joins the pending modifications

The operator stops modification mode briefly, opens the variables panel on the parent instance and adds the variable there, then restarts modification mode so the new scope inherits the variable on apply
The operator opens the source BPMN file in Web Modeler, declares the variable in a new scope, redeploys the model, and Operate then mirrors the variable into the newly generated scope of the instance
The operator picks the new scope and chooses Inject Variable from a context menu, which writes the variable straight into the engine state and bypasses the pending modifications summary view

a.text: Correct. The docs say the operator selects the new scope from the instance history, clicks Add Variable in the variables panel, fills Name and Value, and once the field is blurred with valid values the new variable joins the pending modifications.

b.text: Incorrect. Variables on a newly generated scope are configured inside modification mode on that scope itself, not by exiting modification mode and editing the parent instance variables.

c.text: Incorrect. Adding a variable to a new scope is an Operate modification action; redeploying the BPMN file from Web Modeler does not retroactively inject variables into existing scopes.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no Inject Variable context-menu action; the documented flow uses Add Variable, and the entry is staged as a pending modification rather than written straight into engine state.

explanation: When modification mode creates a new scope, the operator selects that scope in the instance history, clicks Add Variable in the variables panel, fills Name and Value, and blurs the field. The valid entry then joins the pending modifications before being applied.
